response:
The area of the regular hexagonal community center is calculated by dividing the shape into six equilateral triangles with side lengths of 30 meters each, where the area of one triangle is found using the formula involving the square of the side length multiplied by the square root of three and divided by four, resulting in a total hexagonal area of approximately 2338.27 square meters when all six triangles are summed together.

The circular garden inscribed within the hexagon touches the midpoints of the sides, meaning its radius equals the apothem of the hexagon which is derived from the side length multiplied by the square root of three and divided by two, yielding a radius of about 25.98 meters, and the area of this circle is determined by squaring this radius and multiplying by pi to find a final garden area of approximately 2120.58 square meters.
